India · Technology Diplomacy

India's Technology Diplomacy Dashboard

One explorable view of the technology agreements India has signed with partner nations — filter by domain, read the map, and drill into what each agreement actually says. Agreement density reflects diplomatic activity; it may not indicate who leads in a given technology.

Nothing shows until you click.

Select a country on the map to open its agreements. The map stays clean at rest by design — with dozens of partners and eight domains, showing every detail at once would be unreadable. Each agreement then expands to full detail.

What this is not

Scoped honestly, on purpose

The dataset is a credible, sourced sample rather than a claim of completeness. Three limits are worth stating up front so the figures are read for what they are.

C.01

Not full global coverage. The map is populated where verifiable agreements exist across a defined set of partners, not for every nation.

C.02

Not a complete census. It captures a sourced sample of agreements, not every instrument India has ever signed.

C.03

Not a measure of capability. Density reflects diplomatic activity; it may not show who is ahead in any given technology.

Coverage note will populate here.

Source records compiled from Ministry of External Affairs treaty listings, ministry press releases (MeitY, DAE, ISRO, DRDO), parliamentary answers, and think-tank and news reporting. Where a field was unrecorded in the source, it appears as "not recorded" rather than being inferred. Status labels were normalised into active, concluded, lapsed and proposed buckets; the original wording is retained per agreement.

Prepared for the Takshashila Institution. Figures may change as records are verified and extended.
